Our File No.
A-92—539
Page
2
elected officer,
except as specifically permitted by another
exception under subsection
(b)
of the regulation.
We also
advised,
however,
that a general reference to the Franchise Tax
Board is not a reference to an individual board member.
Therefore,
a letter sent to taxpayers at public expense signed by
the “Franchise Tax Board” would not violate Section 89001.
In the Goldberg advice letter (supra),
we further advised
that the “letterhead” exception does not permit the inclusion of
the name of the official in the text of the tax booklet,
which
includes the instructions and other forms or schedules in the tax
booklet, unless another exception applies.
Consequently, the page
you provided for our review which includes a letter from the
Franchise Tax Board to taxpayers within the text of the tax
booklet does not meet the requirements of Regulation 18901(b) (1).
The inclusion of the names of the officials within the text
constitutes
a reference or inference to the elected official.
You subsequently asked whether the same letter could be
printed as a separate page in the tax booklet,
which includes as
part of the standard letterhead or logotype,
the names and titles
of the elected officials.
As also advised in the Goldberg advice
letter
(supra)
,
the names of the officials may appear on the cover
page,
inside cover page,
or elsewhere in the booklet,
provided the
names appear only once and on the standard letterhead or logotype
of the agency stationery,
forms and envelopes.
Therefore, all of
the following must apply:
1.
The names of the elected officers appear in the standard
letterhead or logotype of the agency;
2.
The standard letterhead or logotype of the agency appears
on the standard stationery,
forms and envelopes of the agency;
and
3.
The mass mailing is not otherwise prohibited because of
additional references to the elected officers.
Provided all these requirements are met,
inclusion of the
names and titles of the officials,
which identify the Franchise
Tax Board members as the chairman or a member,
in the letter you
provided for our review is permitted.
(Miller Advice Letter,
No.
1—90-138 and Pointkowski Advice Letter,
No.
A—89—652.)
Please
note that in order to be consistent with the scope of Section
89001 and with our prior practice of narrowly construing


Our File No.
A-92-539
Page
3
exceptions to Regulation 18901,
any further determination of
inclusion of exempted items must be determined on a case—by—case
basis.
If I can be of any further assistance to you,
please feel
free to contact me at
(916)
322—590l.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Luisa Nenchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of l0c per page.

sections.question: You have reque5 confirmation of telephone advice provided to you o October 15, 1992, and OCtober 16, 1992, Ofl behalf of the Franchise Tax Board (the "Boardrr) Concerning the application f Government Code Section 890011 to the members of the Board. This letter confirms my advice SPecifically you asked on October 15, 1992, whether it is permissible to include within the various tax booklets published by the Franchise Tax Board a letter to taxpayers signed by the 11 Franchjse Tax Board " which does not contain the names or any other reference to individual board members. You subsequently asked whether the same letter could be printed as a separate page in the tax booklet, which includes as part of the standard letterhead or logotype, the names and titles of the elected officials.
sections.conclusion: Provided all these requirements are met, inclusion of the names and titles of the officials, which identify the Franchise Tax Board members as the chairman or a member, in the letter you provided for our review is permitted. Please note that in order to be consistent with the scope of Section 89001 and with our prior practice of narrowly construing exceptions to Regulation 18901, any further determination of inclusion of exempted items must be determined on a case-by-case basis.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the Franchise Tax Board include a letter to taxpayers signed by the board name without individual board members' names in tax booklets, and can this letter be printed on a separate page with board members' names and titles in the standard letterhead?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A letter signed by the "Franchise Tax Board" without individual names does not violate Section 89001. Board members' names may appear on a separate page provided they are part of the standard letterhead or logotype that appears only once on the cover, inside cover, or elsewhere following regulatory requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document has OCR quality issues (e.g., 'reque5' for 'request', 'OCtober' for 'October'). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers present; sections identified from content structure. Confirms prior telephone advice. References multiple prior advice letters (A-92-474, Goldberg letter, Miller A-90-138, Pointkowski A-89-652).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have reque5 confirmation of telephone advice provided to you o October 15, 1992, and OCtober 16, 1992, Ofl behalf of the Franchise Tax Board (the "Boardrr) Concerning the application f Government Code Section 890011 to the members of the Board. This letter confirms my advice SPecifically you asked on October 15, 1992, whether it is permissible to include within the various tax booklets published by the Franchise Tax Board a letter to taxpayers signed by the 11 Franchjse Tax Board " which does not contain the names or any other reference to individual board members. You subsequently asked whether the same letter could be printed as a separate page in the tax booklet, which includes as part of the standard letterhead or logotype, the names and titles of the elected officials.

CONCLUSION: Provided all these requirements are met, inclusion of the names and titles of the officials, which identify the Franchise Tax Board members as the chairman or a member, in the letter you provided for our review is permitted. Please note that in order to be consistent with the scope of Section 89001 and with our prior practice of narrowly construing exceptions to Regulation 18901, any further determination of inclusion of exempted items must be determined on a case-by-case basis.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms prior telephone advice regarding Government Code Section 89001 and whether the Franchise Tax Board may include board member names in tax booklets. The advice concludes that a letter signed generically by the board is permissible, and board member names may appear on separate pages as part of standard letterhead if specific regulatory requirements are met.
